uplink.description: fPort 1 status report — normal metering, sensing, no leak
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds bytes; expected.data computed by running reference/upstream-codec.js (totalVolume 123456 L, battRecovered 3480 mV -> 3.48 V, waterTemp min 4/max 10 C, ambient 6 C) converted to vocabulary units.

uplink.description: fPort 1 status report — large leak, reverse-flow error, not sensing, low battery
uplink._note: Synthetic; reference decode: totalVolume 9876543 L, leakState 4 (large) -> water.leak true, errorCode 384 -> Reverse flow, error bit 0x8000 set -> not sensing, battRecovered 3080 mV <= 3100 -> Low battery.

uplink.description: fPort 6 response wrapping a status report — normal metering
uplink._note: Synthetic; fPort-6 response = 3-byte header (echoed fPort, status ok=0, type statusReport=1) + the same 28-byte status report as the first vector. Reference responseDecoder confirms status 'ok' / type 'statusReport'.

uplink.description: fPort 6 response wrapping a hardware report — diagnostics, no metering
uplink._note: Synthetic; reference hardwareReportDecoder yields firmware 1.2.3, appState 'metering', pipe id 1 'Copper 15 mm'. Diagnostics-only frame: no metering.water.total (other vectors satisfy the water-meter requirement).
Reference statusReportDecoder throws 'Wrong payload length (...), should be 28 bytes' for a non-28-byte status report.
Response type 9 is not in responseTypes; reference responseDecoder throws 'Invalid response type: 9'.
Reference decodeUplink only handles fPort 1 and 6 in its switch; we surface unsupported ports as an error.
